title: Pilosella fulgens
intro: Pilosella fulgens, also known as Red Clover or Crimson Clover, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) that thrives in temperate regions with rich soil and moderate temperatures. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including menopausal symptoms, digestive problems, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella fulgens include isoflavones, particularly formononetin and genistein, as well as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pilosella fulgens is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to alleviate symptoms associated with menopause, such as hot flashes and night sweats, as well as to support cardiovascular health and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Pilosella fulgens is classified in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. It belongs to the genus Pilosella, which consists of various species of plants that are native to the Northern Hemisphere. Within the genus, Pilosella fulgens is further grouped as a member of the subgenus Eunomius. This classification system helps to organize and understand the relationships between different plant species. The classification of Pilosella fulgens is also influenced by its evolutionary history and genetic characteristics.
uses: Pilosella fulgens, also known as the sticky mouse-ear, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been employed to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin irritations, and gastrointestinal problems. Its extracts have been studied for their possible antiviral and antifungal activities, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. Additionally, Pilosella fulgens has been used as a treatment for certain types of infections and as an antiseptic agent. More research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential applications.
benefits: Pilosella fulgens, a species of flowering plant, is known to have various health benefits due to its rich content of tannins and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin problems. Its antimicrobial properties have been shown to be effective in reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Additionally, Pilosella fulgens contains antioxidants that can help protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. properties: Pilosella fulgens is known for its rich tradition in herbal medicine, with reported uses including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant's leaves and flowers are said to be used in traditional remedies for various ailments, including fever and digestive issues. However, more research is needed to fully understand the extent of its therapeutic potential. Some studies suggest that Pilosella fulgens may have antimicrobial and hepatoprotective effects, although these findings require further verification. constituents: Pilosella fulgens contains a range of active constituents, including sesquiterpene lactones and other compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties. These constituents have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to explain the plant's traditional uses. The exact composition of Pilosella fulgens can vary depending on the specific species, growing conditions, and other factors. parts: Pilosella fulgens, a species of flowering plant, contains several medicinal parts. The leaves and roots of Pilosella fulgens are used in herbal medicine. preparations: Pilosella fulgens is primarily used in traditional herbal medicine due to its potential anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Pilosella fulgens typically involve the plant's leaves and flowers being dried, then made into teas or infusions. The plant is also sometimes used topically to treat minor skin irritations and wounds. Some studies suggest that Pilosella fulgens may have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. side_effects:
Pilosella fulgens is known to cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly when the plant's sap comes into contact with the skin. 

Ingestion of Pilosella fulgens can lead to gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and vomiting. 

The plant's sap may cause skin redness, itching, and blistering in some people. 

While Pilosella fulgens is not typically life-threatening, it can cause discomfort and may require medical attention in severe cases. 

It is essential to handle the plant with care and avoid touching the sap to minimize the risk of adverse reactions.
